summary: I was the chief of interpretation at the Statue of Liberty during the September 11 attacks. When the attacks happened, we quickly set up a triage area to assist injured visitors and staff. We faced many challenges, including managing the walking wounded and ensuring everyone was accounted for while coordinating with multiple agencies. The heightened security meant closing certain areas and working closely with law enforcement to maintain safety on the island.

On September 11, 2001, the attacks profoundly affected our operations at the Statue of Liberty. We had to respond quickly by setting up emergency triage and coordinating with EMS and law enforcement. The event caused fear and confusion among visitors and staff, but everyone cooperated to follow instructions. It also led to a significant increase in security measures and highlighted the need for better communication and emergency protocols.

During the crisis at the Statue of Liberty on September 11, we had to coordinate closely with the New Jersey State Police. Communication with their troopers was essential but challenging due to overwhelmed systems. They played a key role in managing security and assisting with the evacuation and triage process. Their presence helped us maintain order amid the confusion and heightened threat levels.

Emergency medical services were crucial to our response at the Statue of Liberty on September 11. We worked alongside EMS teams to provide care for injured visitors and staff in the triage area we set up. Despite overwhelmed communication systems, EMS personnel were vital in managing the medical needs during the evacuation. Their collaboration helped save lives and maintain calm during a chaotic time.

The United States Park Police were an important partner in maintaining security at the Statue of Liberty after the September 11 attacks. We worked closely with them to restrict access to certain areas and respond to security threats, including a speedboat scare near the harbor. Their vigilance and coordination with other agencies helped ensure the safety of visitors and staff during a very tense period. This experience underscored the importance of interagency cooperation in crisis situations.
